article_meta.title: Cartoon Carousel The nation’s cartoonists on the week in politics Every week political cartoonists throughout the countr

reasoning: No attribution violations and several genuine author-prose bias instances were identified, but many clear instances in the author’s voice were missed and most labels were nonstandard or misapplied relative to the defined types.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: All flagged passages are from the author’s prose; no quoted or clearly attributed language was flagged.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Flagged items are largely defensible as author-level bias (sweeping claims, loaded descriptors, partisan framing). One item about omission is somewhat speculative but still arguable.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Missed multiple strong instances in author prose: headline/subhead (‘You can thank this woman,’ ‘real architect,’ ‘purges’), overgeneralizations (‘no one is acknowledging’), subjective descriptors (‘ignominious death,’ ‘humiliated Joe,’ ‘wild accusation,’ ‘bridge too far for most Americans’), and causal/intent claims.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Used non-listed or imprecise categories (e.g., Ideological Bias, False Equivalence, Partisan Framing). More appropriate labels from the allowed list would include Unsubstantiated Claims, Opinion as Fact, Slant, Word Choice, and Subjective Qualifying Adjectives.
